Summary:
High-level walk-through of Crimson's vendor security questionnaire (SIG + vendor-specific controls). Focus areas: SOC 2 evidence, KMS/customer-managed keys, VPC/private deployment options, audit log forwarding and retention, data residency EU vs. global, RBAC/SSO provisioning (SAML), and evidence delivery plan. Agreed to share Redwood CAIQ baseline + SOC 2 Type II within one week and schedule a technical KMS deep-dive.

Topics covered:

text: High-level review of admin telemetry: what events Redwood captures by default, export/stream options (S3, webhooks, SIEM connectors), retention controls, encryption/KMS handling for exported archives, and implications for Aurora Medics' SOC2/ISO controls and data residency requirements. title: Cryptographic architecture & HSM onboarding — Aurora HealthTech security sync
preview: Customer is evaluating Redwood for hosting PHI-bearing inference workloads. Primary concerns: BYOK/HSM integration, key rotation & split-key policy, audit log fidelity, data residency, and SSO/RBAC. SE walked through envelope encryption, key wrapping, KMIP vs cloud KMS, and architecture options for private deploymen...
text: Customer is evaluating Redwood for hosting PHI-bearing inference workloads. Primary concerns: BYOK/HSM integration, key rotation & split-key policy, audit log fidelity, data residency, and SSO/RBAC. SE walked through envelope encryption, key wrapping, KMIP vs cloud KMS, and architecture options for private deployment with HSM. Next actions: share HSM onboarding guide, run a 2-week pilot with CloudHSM, provide SOC 2 / ISO artifacts, and schedule a hands-on key rotation tabletop with Aurora infra team.

title: Go/No-Go: Dedicated staged rollouts (beta) + incident review + launch checklist
preview: Internal go/no-go for enabling Dedicated staged rollouts for a limited beta (1–2 design partners). Reviewed early beta failures (429 spike due to canary partition starving effective capacity, gate evidence missing due to telemetry tag mismatch, and metrics cardinality regression). Aligning on ship criteria: feature ...
text: Internal go/no-go for enabling Dedicated staged rollouts for a limited beta (1–2 design partners). Reviewed early beta failures (429 spike due to canary partition starving effective capacity, gate evidence missing due to telemetry tag mismatch, and metrics cardinality regression). Aligning on ship criteria: feature flag default OFF, validated rollback/abort semantics, stable gate evaluation under telemetry lag, and audit export readiness. Decision: GO for limited beta with guardrails and explicit hold/force-promote controls restricted to admin roles; no broad enablement until SIEM export and small-pool safeguards are verified.

[00:00] Ethan Park: Hey everyone, thanks for blocking time. Quick intro — I'm Ethan, AE on the Redwood team. Maya is our solutions engineer, she'll do a quick walkthrough.

text:
Meeting header: 2025-03-05 15:00 UTC | Duration: 46 minutes | Recording: internal/ff/ff-20250305-acme.mp3
Attendees: Ethan Park (Redwood AE), Sofia Martinez (Redwood SE), Rajiv Patel (Redwood CSM); Dana Ruiz (AcmeCloud VP Eng), Liam O'Connor (SRE Lead), Priya Nair (Security), Tom Becker (Procurement)

Summary:
- Purpose: walk through root cause of Mar 3 outage, confirm mitigations, agree next steps and timelines for reliability improvements.
- High-level root cause identified as cascading memory pressure in the batching service + an edge-case in autoscaler thresholds; KV cache thrash amplified fault domain.
- Short-term mitigations: tuned autoscaler, emergency patch to batching memory accounting, temporary traffic shaping / rate-limit policy.
- Long-term actions: circuit breaker pattern, better observability around prefix/K V cache hits, engineer runbooks and chaos tests.

Topics: tim...

title: Technical deep dive — safe canary rollouts on Redwood Hosted
preview: Northstar evaluated Redwood Hosted canary rollout workflow requirements for safely introducing new model versions/configs. Discussion focused on percentage traffic splitting, deterministic cohorting/stickiness, guardrails (max step size, soak time, approvals), auto-stop/rollback conditions based on SLO metrics, and ...
text: Northstar evaluated Redwood Hosted canary rollout workflow requirements for safely introducing new model versions/configs. Discussion focused on percentage traffic splitting, deterministic cohorting/stickiness, guardrails (max step size, soak time, approvals), auto-stop/rollback conditions based on SLO metrics, and audit logging/retention/SIEM export. Redwood covered expected GA workflow in Console, underlying routing enforcement, failure modes (streaming/retries), and operational runbooks/alerts. Customer asked for evidence around change control (who approved what), and how rollouts interact with existing fallback policies and multi-region behavior.

title: Dedicated upgrade safety – staged rollouts / gates / rollback semantics (technical deep dive)
preview: Lattice AI asked for a detailed walkthrough of how Redwood will do safer Dedicated pool upgrades using staged rollouts. Redwood described the rollout plan model (stages, gates, promote/abort/hold) and emphasized deterministic, auditable actions with automatic abort/rollback on gate failure. The group focused on how ...
text: Lattice AI asked for a detailed walkthrough of how Redwood will do safer Dedicated pool upgrades using staged rollouts. Redwood described the rollout plan model (stages, gates, promote/abort/hold) and emphasized deterministic, auditable actions with automatic abort/rollback on gate failure. The group focused on how stages map to reserved capacity (traffic splitting vs replica partitions), avoiding starving small pools during canary, and how to handle missing/noisy metrics. Lattice AI emphasized strict change management (audit logs, RBAC, reason codes), and asked about rollback semantics for streaming and multi-region behavior if one region degrades mid-stage.

title: POC scoping: route NLP model shortlist - Horizon Transit
preview: Discovery + POC scoping to pick an open-model shortlist for multilingual route extraction and scheduling heuristics. Focus on accuracy on Spanish/Portuguese route names, 100–300ms p50 latency target for interactive UI, and pilot timeline of ~8 weeks after bench validation.
text: Discovery + POC scoping to pick an open-model shortlist for multilingual route extraction and scheduling heuristics. Focus on accuracy on Spanish/Portuguese route names, 100–300ms p50 latency target for interactive UI, and pilot timeline of ~8 weeks after bench validation.
